page_content:
SLLC386 Experiential Learning (3 Credits) Prerequisite: Learning Proposal approved by the Ofﬁce of Experiential Learning Programs, faculty sponsor and the student's internship sponsor. Restriction: Junior standing or higher.

SLLC400 Articulatory Phonetics for Second Language Acquisition and Application (3 Credits) The mechanical capabilities of the human vocal apparatus for producing speech sounds, and their terminology and transcription in the International Phonetic Alphabet. Emphasis is on the practical needs of the teacher and student of foreign language, rather than the theoretical linguist or the hearing-and-speech pathologist. The phonetics of major languages are also introduced, with attention to the pedagogy of their phonetics. Restriction: Junior standing or higher.

SLLC410 Documentary and Narrative (3 Credits) An examination of the relationship between ﬁlm and reality, focusing on documentary ﬁlm. Prerequisite: ENGL245, FILM245, or CINE245; or permission of ARHU- School of Languages, Literatures, and Cultures department. Cross-listed with: CINE410. Credit Only Granted for: SLLC410, CINE410 or FILM410. Formerly: FILM410.
